comments.author: @GLAVNIO
comments.text:
Timeline Discrepancy
Treaty of Bucharest: Signed on August 10, 1913.  This treaty ended the Second Balkan War, formally assigning Thessalonica (and the region of Macedonia) to Greece. 
Jekyll Island Meeting: The secret meeting often called the "birth" of the Federal Reserve took place from November 22 to November 30, 1910.  This is where the initial blueprint (the Aldrich Plan) was drafted.
Federal Reserve Act: The legislation was signed into law by President Woodrow Wilson on December 23, 1913, more than four months after the Treaty of Bucharest.

comments.text: Nice video, but I would like to add a small historical correction. For over 13 centuries, Bulgaria wasn't just a country that stood at the crossroads of the Roman Empires. comments.author: @venimus777
comments.text: Btw the "opposite shaking" is not true. Bulgarian head movements are not the same at all. First the "no" is not "nodding". It's a single soft tilting the head back, focusing on the lifting the chin up. Nodding is more energetic and repetitive moving the chin down. It's clear shaking, not really the Bulgarian single move gesture. Then the "yes" is also not the rotating face movement you call "shaking". Bulgarians tilt the neck left and right, shaking is in a different axis. Face is always forward, the tip of the head moves left and right, it's not head rotation, it's tilting. Also these gestures are quite common among multiple countries. One really typical is India, and another is Turkey where those were actually assimilated from in Bulgaria. It's probably confusing for foreigners, but not because are the opposite but because they are completely different and unknown to them.

comments.author: @mariapopova5027
comments.text: You are so wrong. It's not survival that defines us. It's greatness!  You didn't even mention rhe First and the Second Bulgarian Empire that were big and mighty. We conquered Byzanz many times and spread our territory on three seas. Bulgarian Emperors once were feared and respected. We invented the Kyrillic alphabet which is now the third official Christian  alphabet in the world besides Latin and Greek. But you never mention those important facts in order to make us appear random and paradoxical.
